summary: Converted Shy Guy/Heiho item code (`itheiho`) from assembly to matching C and began decompiling food item support (`itfoods`). The PR removed `asm/melee/it/items/itheiho.s`, added `src/melee/it/items/itheiho.c`, switched the build/config entries to compile it as matching C, introduced item-var structs for `heiho` and `foods`, and moved/prototyped related food/freezie/MS bomb item functions into narrower headers. The PR body explicitly notes one remaining non-matching function: `it_8028FBE4` in `itfoods.c`.
what_changed: Deleted the 1551-line assembly file `asm/melee/it/items/itheiho.s` and added a 432-line C implementation at `src/melee/it/items/itheiho.c`.

Added a partial `src/melee/it/items/itfoods.c` implementation with food item state table code and functions including `it_8028F9D8`, `it_8028FAF4`, `it_8028FBE4`, `it_8028FC5C`, `it_8028FC84`, `it_8028FC8C`, `it_8028FCBC`, `it_8028FCE8`, `it_8028FD44`, `it_8028FD4C`, `it_8028FD50`, `it_8028FD7C`, `it_8028FDD8`, `it_8028FDE0`, `it_8028FDE4`, `it_8028FE0C`, `it_8028FE14`, `it_8028FE44`, and `it_8028FE70`.
Added `itHeiHoVars`, `itFoodsVars`, and `itFoodsAttributes` to `itCommonItems.h`, and added `foods` and `heiho` members to the `Item` union in `src/melee/it/types.h`.
Introduced `src/melee/it/items/itheiho.h` with public prototypes for `it_802D8618`, `it_802D8688`, `it_802D8EC8`, `it_802D9A0C`, and `it_803F83F0`.
Expanded `itfoods.h` to expose food helpers used by `itheiho`, including `it_8028F9D8`, `it_8028FAF4`, and `it_8028FC5C`, and added `it/inlines.h` to that header.

Moved prototypes out of broad `it_27CF.h`: removed `it_8028FAF4`, `it_8028FE90`, and `it_802D8618` from that catch-all item header, then added relevant declarations to `itfoods.h`, `itmsbomb.h`, and `itheiho.h`.

Added missing item helper prototypes in `it_266F.h`, `it_2725.h`, and `itfreeze.h`, including `it_8026DA70`, `it_80273318`, `it_80274ED8`, `it_8027B730`, `it_8028F8E4`, `it_8028F968`, and `it_8028F9B8`.
Updated `grstory.c` to include `it/items/itheiho.h` instead of `it/items/it_27CF.h` for spawning Shy Guys with `it_802D8618`.
Updated `ftGw_SpecialS.c` to include `it/items/itfoods.h`, consistent with food item prototypes being relocated there.

Adjusted `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` so many `itheiho` internal functions became local-scope symbols while public entry points such as `it_802D8618`, `it_802D8688`, `it_802D8EC8`, and `it_802D9A0C` remained global.
smart_moves: Kept the build-system transition explicit: assembly object removed from `obj_files.mk`, C object added, and `configure.py` marked `itheiho.c` as `Matching`.

Added typed item-var structs and wired them through the existing `Item.xDD4_itemVar` union, allowing the C code to express former raw offsets like `0xDD4`/`0xDF8` as `ip->xDD4_itemVar.heiho.x24` and food state as `ip->xDD4_itemVar.foods.x0`/`x4`.

Separated ownership of prototypes into item-specific headers instead of continuing to route unrelated item APIs through `it_27CF.h`; `grstory.c` now includes `itheiho.h`, food users include `itfoods.h`, and MS bomb functions live in `itmsbomb.h`.
Preserved matching-oriented anonymous names where semantics were not proven (`it_802D...`, `x20`, `x24`, etc.) rather than over-naming unknown Shy Guy fields.
Marked internal `itheiho` functions local in `symbols.txt`, reducing exported symbol noise while keeping necessary entry points global.
Documented the remaining non-matching food function in the PR body, making the partial status clear.
decomp_lessons: When converting an item from asm to C, update all three matching surfaces together: delete the asm file, switch `obj_files.mk` to the C object, and mark the source as `Object(Matching, ...)` in `configure.py`.
For item-specific `xDD4_itemVar` work, add a named union member and minimal offset-preserving struct before replacing raw item-var offsets in C.
Do not keep unrelated item function declarations in broad catch-all headers once item-specific headers exist; move declarations to the narrow header that owns the item subsystem.

Partial support files can still be useful if they unblock a matching target; here, partial `itfoods.c` supplied helpers used by matched `itheiho.c`, while explicitly leaving `it_8028FBE4` non-matching.
Scope cleanup in `symbols.txt` is part of decomp hygiene: functions only called within the new C file can become local, while cross-file entry points remain global.
For stage-spawned items, update stage code includes to the item-specific public header rather than relying on aggregator headers.
naming_conventions: Unresolved functions kept address-based names such as `it_802D8618`, `it_802D8688`, `it_802D8EC8`, `it_802D9A0C`, `it_8028F9D8`, and `it_8028FAF4`.
Item variable structs used subsystem-specific typedef names `itHeiHoVars`, `itFoodsVars`, and `itFoodsAttributes`.
Unknown struct fields retained offset-style names (`x20`, `x21`, `x24`, `x2C`, `x54`) rather than guessed semantic names.
The `Item` union gained lowercase item-var members `foods` and `heiho`, matching existing `xDD4_itemVar.<item>` access style.
Public header prototypes include address comments like `/* 2D8618 */ void it_802D8618(...)`, matching existing project convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the full `itheiho.s` assembly translation unit with C and switched build references to the C object.
Used `PAD_STACK` in functions where stack shape needed preserving, including evidence in `itfoods.c` (`it_8028FBE4`, `it_8028FCE8`) and `itheiho.c` (`it_802D8688`).

Used explicit casts and exact types in spawn/helper paths, for example `(s8)` assignment into `heiho` fields, `(HSD_GObj*)` casts around item object calls, and `HSD_GObj*` return typing for `it_8028FAF4`.
Matched item animation/state behavior through `ItemStateTable` definitions and repeated `Item_80268E5C(..., ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE)` calls.
Preserved floating-point constants visible in the matched logic, including `1.0F`, `2.0F`, `3.1415927F`, `1.5F`, `0x3C0`/`960`, and timer constants like `0x14` and `0xC`.

Used existing item and HSD helpers (`GET_ITEM`, `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj`, `HSD_JObjSetRotationY`, `HSD_JObjSetTranslate`, `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub`, `HSD_AObjSetFlags`) instead of open-coding object manipulation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the supplied PR slice.
The only explicit human note in the PR text is: `Non-matching functions: it_8028FBE4 in itfoods.c`.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR that matches remaining non-matching `it_8028FBE4` in `itfoods.c`.
Search for subsequent renames or semantic field naming for `itHeiHoVars` fields `x20`, `x21`, `x22`, `x24`, `x2C`, and `x54`.
Check whether `itfoods.c` later became a fully matching object in `configure.py`.
Look for follow-up cleanup of the commented-out `it_802D8618` call left in `grstory.c`.
Compare later PRs that moved more declarations out of `it_27CF.h` into item-specific headers.
